body:
- Closes #47524
- Closes https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/issues/47574
- Closes #47579
- In most cases, before Hibernate validation, we have the check of whether the payload and path `clientId` are the same - if it's missing, it fails - but it's still good to mark it as required for OpenAPI spec + be more defensive for some possible cases like [this](https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/compare/main...mabartos:KC-47524?expand=1#diff-6c00855e2a8dcb4913c2b9a4f34360421beaf24d927382e759fe16474e27f883R170)

comments.body: While I didn't know about https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/issues/47574, I already handled it in my PR opened before this one. Let's go with your version as you have linked actual opened issue.
@michalvavrik Ahh, didn't know about that. Whatever you prefer.
@mabartos As discussed today during the WG meeting, can you please also make `protocol` required?

title: [client-v2] Require 'clientId' in the payload
